strengths: Nearly 20 years of engineering experience spanning web, backend, embedded, and distributed systems, including low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic — directly relevant to the event-streaming and real-time-inference demands of the role.

Current hands-on work at re:cinq building AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and frameworks supporting agentic workflows, plus founder-level ownership of GISCollective, an open-source event-driven platform he has architected and operated since 2016.

Meaningful fintech and regulated-domain exposure: investment-platform engineering at RIDE Capital (Germany) with disciplined craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and multi-country pricing systems at Optilyz — evidence of production rigor in financially consequential systems.
concerns: No demonstrated executive or formal engineering-leadership tenure; career history is individual-contributor and staff-level roles, with no documented track record scaling teams from 10 to 30+ or developing a technical-leadership layer.
Agentic AI experience is recent (since April 2025) and tooling-oriented; the resume does not evidence operating production multi-agent systems with financial consequences at scale.
No stated experience governing outsourced/hybrid engineering delivery models or presenting technical roadmaps to boards, both explicit requirements of the role.

key_factors.factor: Hands-on agentic-AI infrastructure work (MCP, agent frameworks)

response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings an unusually broad and durable engineering foundation — nearly two decades across high-throughput distributed systems, event-driven platforms, and regulated fintech products — combined with current, hands-on work at the frontier of agentic AI infrastructure, precisely the technical substrate this CTO role demands. His founder experience running GISCollective end-to-end demonstrates architectural ownership, product judgment, and long-horizon operational responsibility beyond typical IC scope, while his craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, observability, testing tooling he authored himself) maps directly onto the role's emphasis on evaluation rigor and production reliability. For a scale-up seeking a technically credible leader who can personally architect a model-agnostic multi-agent platform rather than manage from altitude, his profile represents a strong, defensible fit on the technical dimensions that matter most.
